name: Altai Krai (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the rivers and lakes of the Altai
courage, valour, and the historic Russian state
wheat, fertility, and the agricultural wealth of the krai
the snowy peaks of the Altai mountains

description: A red field with a narrow blue stripe along the hoist; the blue stripe contains a stylised yellow sheaf of wheat above the krai's emblem.
history: The flag of Altai Krai was officially adopted on 28 September 2000 by the Altai Krai Council of People's Deputies.

features.symbolism: the blue hoist stripe represents the rivers and lakes of the Altai region; the red field draws on the historic banner of the Russian Empire and recalls the courage of the krai's people.

features.role: sheaf of wheat with stylised steam-engine smokestack
features.symbolism: a sheaf of wheat over a stylised emblem of industry; recalls the agricultural and industrial economy of Altai Krai.
